I went for a heavy tech hero synergy and was not disappointed. I was able to capture some extra villains using Punisher’s Hail of Bullets card. Very tough set up.

We were not sure which heroes to select, so I read the tournament channel and someone suggested using Angel and Mirage. It was a great suggestion! Even if the game was a bit long and we did not get much VPs, it was very fun using the discard mechanic for great effects (we had a turn with 31 attack!).
Go Blue Team!!!

Well that was hard work. In both games we had runs of turns where Strikes would mean we could do nothing at all, and in game 1 this led to escapes. I had managed to get Angel working with Photon in game 1, discarding Diving Catch repeatedly to gain bystanders. Once I got Jean's Rare I finished off Apocalypse. Game 2 I just had Photon so made a Hyperspeed deck that was coming up just short of 12 on several turns. In both games my wife was recruiting and attacking Sidekicks to keep us from losing. She felt hard done by in this one, hence the low fun rating.
As an aside, I spent the entire time prepping for this thinking Mirage was the preselected hero instead of Karma and based my selections around that! It worked, but I think I could have made better use of the pre chosen heroes had I thought a little harder.

We went with the Ancient one and Joe Fix-It hoping to be able to use recruit as an attack method for villains and the MM and also maybe pulling in the strength with tech of heroes provided for us, Also maybe having a few shards in there - if we failed the first game we were going to go for a Shard strategy the 2nd game with our heroes. We had decided pre-game that we would probably knock Dr Strange out first - if that was an option and with a Scheme Twist up first he was knocked out straight away.

column36: Fun set up! A classic fav scheme. We added yondu and phyla vel to help maximize shard utility. Adam warlock “matter card” that lets you burn two shards to get a hero at half cost helped us get some 8 boat hero cards early on which ended up getting the work done that needed to be done. :)

I really don't like this Scheme much, it's all too random with the massive hero deck and what makes it into the HQ.  First playthrough had no escapes but took way too long (though interestingly enough, both players had the same exact VP piles) so I'm going with the second run, despite the escapes.

I was thinking about bringing heroes that use danger sense to manipulate the annoying and challenging VD, but I felt like I couldn't really plan my strategies for the deck building because of the scheme, so I just used the hero tier list made from the votes on the server, and picked the two highest rated options I had available. I also used the heroe's tier list ratings to decide on who to pick when the schemes occured. It was really helpful for this game. But the game itself was really challenging! I barely won my first game (next card in the VD would've been the losing scheme), and I lost my second one. One of my smartest moves was to put "Dr. Strange, possessed by Zom" to the Astral plane, so he doesn't get the bonuses from his 3 bystanders, and he also carried a shard which even made Magus weaker (one less villain with a shard in the city).
I am hoping to get to play some of the newer sets on the last week, the newest one for me so far was Black Panther. Can't wait to see the other's results and hero choices!

Tough setup. I used Captain Mar-Vell and Yondu for Danger Sense,  thinking I would push twists to play early and thin out the excess heroes in the HQ. That worked to an extent in the first game, but it was too slow to build up strength. I was able to push the last 2 twists to the end of the deck though, so I lasted as long as possible. I almost won the first game. I was set up to hit the mastermind for the 5th time on turn 40, but on turn 39 I got the tactic that forces you to hit the mastermind with only recruit. Brutal way to end the game. Second game was worse. I wasn't able to get enough attack or danger sense cards to stave off a loss for as long.

I was hoping my hero choices would make things more interesting, but I think because they were a bit on the weaker side they made the game feel like it was dragging out.

Both games were a slog. The shard management also contributed to the lack of fun. Nothing felt like it would work fast enough to even manage the city as everything grew so fast and even when we would start to manage the city something would happen to fill the city or wreck our hands.

Ravage is a beast.  I got his engine going early in the game and with his villain deck detection and Yondu's Danger Sense, I was able to time the strikes and schemes well and kept rolling up high VP villains until they were going to kick in.  My favorite tourney round so far.
Cyber mod blue and black ftw

Seemed a bit easy.  Maybe just a good draw with lots of winter soldier draw and the big agent Venom, dr Punisher (and eventually Winter Soldier) cards being easily recruited early.  Lots of cars draw thanks to W.S. And Dr Punisher.  I liked the scheme a lot, but the alchemex adapting masterminds were kind of underwhelming because they always had tech cards in the escape pile so only one or their modifiers ever triggered in a really negative way.

The first game I was able to get Ghost Rider's rare early to continue my tech engine. I got really lucky in the villain deck to only show two Master Strikes, one of which happened after the Alchemex was gone. Though Scheme Twists I was less fortunate with as Proxima Midnight became a Mastermind fairly early.

With Ghost Rider's Rare and a good Tech/Strength deck with the other side being Covert with a splash of range for shards. I was able to get a good mix on taking enemies out on both sides rather effectively. I did take a bit longer on turns because of all the Masterminds, mostly Proxima Midnight but felt good actually getting the win where it could have been trouble.

This was a great setup that I really enjoyed. Excellent synergy between the Villains, Mastermind, Keywords and the Scheme. I found good synergy too with one side going heavy on Tech and the other with Ranged. Both Doctor Doom 2099 and Nova fit well with the other heroes. Some timely cards like Ghost Rider 2099's rare and Yondu's burning of shards really helped keep the city clean...which meant that the Twists would whiff. Danger Sense was such an important keyword for keeping me one step ahead of the villains...loved it. I don't think I'll be the fastest this week...but I think I did well.

We got our butts handed to us in the first game. We had several scheme twist and master strikes come out and lost due to six masterminds. The second game we had a little bit more time to work with and we were better able to manage the villains. Ghostrider was our main strategy with one deck and the other deck used winter soldier, Yondu and beast. The hands worked really well and we were able to get high damage quick.

Second game, didn't do cyber-mods at for myself.  Focused on shards. Focused and keeping city empty.  Managed to get 2 Twists with no villains on the board.  It was a hard fight but managed to win.

column36: Halfway through the match we decided to go for the power corruption strategy, but when we were chipping away at Dormammu for VP it was the same turn count to not be corrupted at the Dormammu's last 6VP card. Mockingbird and Deathlok got things going for AntDude and had SHIELD level things working, and WaspWoman was a shard machine with Gamora and Rocket. The setup I submitted started poorly, with expensive cards in the HQ and big villains in the City, and I allowed three escapes before I managed to defeat so much as a Henchman... however, once it started rolling, it really rolled and in the end I'm pretty pleased with how the deck performed (even though I'm sure my escapes will put me low in the rankings this week).

column36: The heroes split into two clear decks, one with strength cards and one with instinct. Right hand had 4 drax artifacts giving consistent +4 attack and 3 rares, even having a turn where I had to waste 23 attack because enemies could only be fought with recruit. The other deck was pretty strong as well though, mostly instinct cards which could be all drawn with moon knights 5 cost. Overall very fun game where the decks just worked well!

Mysterio is rewarding mastermind in the sense that when you get to 20+ attack turns you can spend eventually spend that properly (without ending the game early). So it was fun. 
In both game left hand went mostly yellow and green, right hand full black. Now the first game was quite lucky as I spent most of it in normal mode and maybe two turns in negative zone mode. Also all 5 master strikes came up, so could get a high VP. The second game on the other hand was mostly spent in negative zone mode. Just before getting high cost cards scheme 4 arrived so for many rounds I could not even recruit anymore. But by that time I already had some good cards to chip away mysterio with recruit and manage the city. In the end the game ended on same turn, but as only 3 master strikes came up, VP was lower. 
Black hand relied on versatile cards left hand on having a balanced number of recruit and attack cards.

Both games had one side stall for several turns because I built either too much recruit or too much attack. Domino was a good choice I think, but in hindsight I should've have chosen a focus hero for my second pick.

This was a great setup, I've never mixed Hidden Witnesses and Human Shields before, but it was a bonanza of bystanders.  Spider-Gwen's Save the Day was a rockstar, making the final turns brutal to be Mojo.  I may have to try it again for fun using Epic Mojo!

My first time getting to use my new-to-me X-Men expansion! I keyed in on Piercing Energy being important for fighting Mojo and his villains. Even after a solid first game, I wanted to still test out whether I could generate enough Piercing Energy from Lightshow and Dazzler with my second game. I was never in danger of losing my second game, but I was able to test and show myself that Piercing Energy via Dazzler and Lightshow is much less reliable. As an upside though, it's not all-in on Piercing Energy as my first picks were, so it had flexibility for generating fight as well. With this setup though, reliably getting Piercing Energy was so key though to not have to slog through all the Human Shields. I think I was able to regularly get just 5 P.E. to have enough to fight Mojo, but it would be interesting to try again against a MM with 6 victory points. That one extra VP might be enough for Banshsee + Polaris' P.E. to not be reliable enough. I got a little greedy in my first attempt and got lucky with the villain deck. I had the opportunity to hit Mojo with PE or clear a couple villains from the city. I should have paid more attention that the other player's hand only had recruit and wouldn't be able to fight any villains from the city. I luckily avoided a villain escape by the top card being a Master Strike rather than another villain which would have pushed one into escape. Was a case of being greedy and trying to knock out the MM ASAP almost bit me in the rear, as escapes are so much more detrimental to the tournament scoring. Fun setup with LOADS of bystanders and a great way for me to break in my X-Men expansion! Cheers!

Scores were actually 148 for left and 41 for right. 
Very fun setup. Knew you made it to be a bystander bonanza so we tried to lean into it. Spider-gwen showed up hardcore which was what I had hoped. Using her "Save the Day" my wife was averaging around 30 attack towards the end.

I was able to get Spider-Gwen's Save the Day and keep the city pretty clear which made it extremely useful. If I had been able to get 2 or 3 into my deck, it would've been game over for Mojo in near record time. As it was, I did okay. The lack of scheme twists popping up was tough, because I had a bunch of empty recruit and would've loved the chance to rescue some Hidden Witnesses.

That said it was a no-brainer for us to take Spider-Gwen, but we had a small discussion of either Steve Rogers or Silk for our 5th Hero. I'm glad we went with Steve, because my partner's deck which was focused on Steve cards went crazy with rescuing Bystanders. I did ok with Gwen, but we took a while to clear the Rooftops so my progress was halted a bit. Hopefully our scores hold up, but I think everyone will go crazy with their scores for this setup. I hope our confidence with our results is no mirage!

Against all odds and my expectation my first attempt was actually pretty good. Looking at the setup I was kind of angry that two of the worst/hardest henchmen were in this game. Combined with the Mastermind and Scheme, there were only 2-7 cards that would not put pressure on the CIty (2 bystanders and potentially 5 master strikes with a non-empty Bank). With my first card being a Master Strike Thor Corps weren't that easy to defeat anymore (3 troopers at least were not going to cut it). Luckily I chose Heroes that would be able to provide the KO that was sorely lacking from the setup, while also preventing unwanted KOs from Ghost Racers due to being Covert cards.

Deck/Hand 1 got a very good start with some of Apocalpytic Kitty Pride's cards early on, 1 of her 5-cost and 2 of her 4-costs. And also a couple of Black Widow's 3-costs. Deck 2 got some Superior Iron-Man cards. and bought the blue Iron Man cards that showed up and some Tony Stark cards. Deck 1 ended up getting 2 copies of Apocalyptic Kitty Pride's 5-costs and they were very strong, as most cards available were tech. I did end up burying the Ultimates, as I didn't think I could afford them, which turned out to be correct. KOing all starters really drove up the power level of Deck 1. 20-ish attack turns happened from turn 19 and onward, when all starters had been KO'd.

Still with all these good things happening, I could not prevent all escapes. 3 Villains ended up escaping anyway (and all 7 Twists got played as well). The 4th escape was a Wolverinepool, who didn't do any damage (with his Escape effect) and got shuffled back into the Villain Deck, so didn't end up counting as an escape.

Attempt 2 was more like the nightmare I expected looking at the setup. With fewer Scheme Twists, HQ just didn't bring any good cards, or at least I didn't draw them at the same turns and didn't get any KOs going. 6 escapes at turn 20 with only 2 Twists played and not even a chance at building good Decks.

Thematically the setup was pretty cool, with a lot of Iron Man variants against Iron Monger, but technically I could see people disliking it because of the Scheme and Henchmen chosen. Ghost Racers could not be buried by Thor Corps, as they end up in the discard piles and decks and not the VIcotry Pile. This meant that you need a lot of luck and/or strong cards in HQ and preferably both decks to be able to bury the Ghost Racers. Which if you couldn't do it, would potentially threaten multiple escapes at once. They tecnically do have a KO Fight effect, but it's the worst one in the game, lmiting to Attack only and perhaps forcing you to KO actual good cards if you didn't have Troopers left and no Covert to prevent it.

Evil Deadpool Corpse was pretty fun, but the Revenge effect made them tough as well. Iron Foes weren't all that threatening, but with all the pressure on the City, Justin Hammer was actually pretty strong and ended up escaping Twice, along with a Thor Corps member.

I'm curious what will be in store for last week. I hope Bagel wil grant me something cool as Henchmen, like Sentinels, Hydra Base or anything with a normal "KO one of your Heroes". A Big Challenge is fine, as long as we're given the tools to succesfully deal with it :D

First game I got wrecked hard, but we came online fast in game two and the draw power started hitting fast. Most of those escapes all happened on one turn, when a Twist caused RotLD shenanigans, but other than that it all ran pretty clean!

I've never cone so close to losing in two different ways and still manged to win. Which is good enough because I don't have tome for a second attempt. I tried Happy Hogan for the first time since I heard he is really good but couldn't trigger his best cards like wound blocking and KOing a Master Strike. Very stressful game overall, but maybe others chose more strategically. I loved the idea of thematically having Happy Hogan and High Tech Spider-Man helping Iron Man take down Iron Monger even if the synergies didn't always work
